attributes.stem: A 28-year-old woman presents to the antenatal clinic for a routine check-up at 36 weeks gestation. She has no significant medical history and takes only prenatal vitamins. This is her first pregnancy, which has been uncomplicated so far. She reports some reduced fetal movements over the last day. On examination, she appears well. Her pulse rate is 78/min, blood pressure 118/72 mmHg, respiratory rate 16/min, SpO2 98% on room air, and temperature 36.7°C. The fundal height is appropriate for gestational age. A cardiotocograph (CTG) is performed as part of the routine assessment.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following ranges represents the normal fetal baseline heart rate on CTG?
attributes.explanation:
The correct answer is that the normal fetal baseline heart rate on cardiotocography (CTG) is 110-160 beats per minute. This range is considered reassuring and indicative of a well-oxygenated, non-stressed fetus.

The fetal heart rate baseline is one of the key parameters assessed in CTG interpretation. It is defined as the mean level of the fetal heart rate when it is stable, excluding accelerations and decelerations. Understanding the normal range is crucial for identifying potential fetal distress or other complications.

90-160 beats per minute is partially correct but includes rates below the normal range. A baseline below 110 bpm is considered bradycardia and may indicate fetal distress, particularly if persistent.

110-180 beats per minute extends beyond the upper limit of normal. A baseline above 160 bpm is considered tachycardia and may indicate fetal distress, maternal fever, or other complications.

100-150 beats per minute includes rates below the normal range and doesn't extend to the upper limit of normal. This range could miss some normal fetal heart rates and potentially lead to unnecessary interventions.

120-180 beats per minute misses the lower end of the normal range and extends beyond the upper limit. This range could lead to missed diagnoses of bradycardia and overdiagnosis of tachycardia.

It's important to note that while the baseline heart rate is a crucial component of CTG interpretation, it should always be considered in conjunction with other parameters such as variability, accelerations, and decelerations. Any significant deviations from the normal range should prompt further investigation and potentially changes in management to ensure optimal fetal outcomes.
learningPoints: The normal fetal baseline heart rate on cardiotocography (CTG) is 110-160 beats per minute.
Interpretation of fetal heart rate patterns is crucial for assessing fetal well-being during pregnancy and labour.
Deviations from the normal baseline may indicate fetal distress or other complications.
references: Royal Australian and New Zealand College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists (RANZCOG). Intrapartum Fetal Surveillance Clinical Guideline [Internet]. 4th ed. Melbourne: RANZCOG; 2019. Available from: https://ranzcog.edu.au/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/Intrapartum-Fetal-Surveillance.pdf
